title: feat: add nanobanana-2-lite image model
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Adds Nano Banana 2 Lite (Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Image, GA on Vertex) as a new paid image model.

- Registry entry `nanobanana-2-lite` (aliases `nanobanana2lite`, `nanobanana-lite`), paid-only, routing to Vertex `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-image`
- Cost is exactly half of `nanobanana-2`: prompt text/image $0.25/1M, completion text $1.5/1M, completion image $30/1M (1120 tokens/1K image = $0.0336)
- Wired into the Vertex model map and image dispatch switch

Note: Google's docs list 512/1K/2K/4K output, but Vertex returns `400 INVALID_ARGUMENT` on imageSize 2K/4K for this model — it only outputs 1K. Left it out of the `imageSize` allow-list so large requests default to 1K instead of erroring.

Verified end-to-end on local gen against live Vertex: T2I across sizes/aspect ratios, image editing, output cache MISS→HIT, error paths (400 not 5xx), and billing (0.0336 pollen/image, matching Tinybird rows, no missing-rate warnings). Full automated suites pass (enter aliases/pricing/snapshot, gen usage/permissions/image/billing/cache).

gist.headline: A lighter shade of banana
gist.blurb: We've added Nano Banana 2 Lite to the hive! It's half the cost of its bigger sibling and perfect for quick, beautiful 1K image generations.
gist.summary: Added Nano Banana 2 Lite (`nanobanana-2-lite`) as a new paid image model, powered by Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Image on Vertex, costing exactly half of the standard Nano Banana 2.
gist.impact: Users can now generate 1K images at half the price (0.0336 Pollen per image) using the `nanobanana-2-lite` model alias.
gist.visual_concept: The PR introduces a 'Lite' version of the Nano Banana model. Visually, this translates to a smaller, lightweight, or glowing banana. Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot can be examining or celebrating this new, efficient little fruit.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny scientist goggles, carefully examining a small, glowing yellow pixel-art banana resting on a workbench. The chunky Monitor Robot stands nearby, its screen displaying a beautiful 1K resolution landscape. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
